Ad Text:
Senior Change Analyst - Contract - Data Governance & Risk Program

Job Title: Senior Change Analyst
Location: Melbourne (2 days per week onsite)
Contract Length: 6 months initially
Day Rate: $850 per day inc super

About the Role

An exciting opportunity exists for an experienced Senior Change Analyst to join a large-scale Data Governance & Risk transformation program. This role will be instrumental in shaping and delivering change outcomes across complex business, process, data, and technology initiatives.

You will work closely with senior stakeholders, SMEs, and delivery teams to assess impacts, drive readiness, coordinate change activity across multiple streams, and ensure successful adoption of new ways of working. This is a hands-on role suited to someone who can operate autonomously, influence stakeholders, and lead change in a fast-paced program environment.

Requirements

Lead end-to-end change impact assessments across business, process, data, and technology domains
Develop and execute change strategies aligned to broader program objectives
Facilitate workshops with stakeholders, SMEs, and technical teams
Build trusted relationships across business units, technical teams, and leadership groups
Coordinate change activity across multiple workstreams to ensure consistency of messaging and delivery
Deliver business readiness assessments and implementation plans
Produce high-quality change artefacts including communications, training materials, stakeholder plans, and impact assessments
Support training design, capability uplift, transition to BAU, and go-live readiness
Track adoption outcomes and support post-implementation reviews
Identify and manage change risks, issues, and dependencies

Selection Criteria

Strong experience as a Senior Change Analyst, Change Lead, or similar role within large transformation programs
Proven ability to independently deliver impact assessments, readiness activities, and stakeholder engagement strategies
Experience working across data, risk, regulatory, or technology-focused programs
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders
Strong planning, analytical, and problem-solving capability
Experience translating technical change into clear business impacts and actions
Exposure to Data Governance, Data Risk, Data Quality, or Data Strategy programs highly regarded
Understanding of data governance frameworks, data management principles, or enterprise data tools advantageous
Change qualifications such as Prosci highly regarded

Why Apply

This is an opportunity to join a high-profile transformation program delivering meaningful enterprise-wide change. You'll work across strategic initiatives, engage senior stakeholders, and play a key role in improving governance, risk maturity, and business capability. Ideal for a senior change professional seeking a challenging and visible role with strong long-term impact.

If this role is of interest, please apply or email your CV to karim.romdhane@precisionsourcing.com.au and we'll be in touch if your experience matches the role!

Are you passionate about data strategy, architecture, and empowering businesses with data-driven insights? Do you enjoy designing scalable solutions and guiding organizations through complex data challenges? At InterWorks, we’re looking for a Data Architect who thrives in dynamic environments and is ready to help shape the future of data for our clients.

This role blends the rigor of enterprise data architecture and governance with the energy of consulting and solution-building. You’ll work with clients across industries to build robust data platforms, define standards, and implement governance frameworks that create lasting value.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the Melbourne, Australia InterWorks office. Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications.

What You’ll Do

Architect and implement enterprise-level data solutions that align with business goals and compliance requirements.

Design and optimize data models, storage solutions, and integration pipelines across various platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, etc.).

Partner directly with clients to develop scalable data platforms, data governance frameworks, and analytics strategies.

Create and oversee ETL/ELT processes and advise on best practices for data quality, security, and accessibility.

Serve as a strategic liaison between business and IT to bridge gaps and ensure alignment.

Manage project timelines, expectations, and deliverables with clear communication and strong stakeholder engagement.

What You’ll Need

Must-Haves

5+ years of experience in data architecture, data engineering, or a related field

High proficiency in SQL and strong data modeling skills

Experience in data governance, compliance standards, and enterprise data strategy

Excellent communication skills—written, verbal, and interpersonal

Ability to thrive in client-facing environments and lead cross-functional teams

Adaptability and problem-solving mindset in fast-paced and ambiguous situations

Proven experience with data lakehouse architecture including the storage and processing of semi-structured and unstructured file types

Proven support for data science initiatives

Understanding of pipeline and cost monitoring and optimisation

Comfortable leading business engagements to source requirements or refine data models

Experience with Azure & AWS CSP's preferable

What We’d Like You to Have

Experience with BI and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)

Familiarity with modern data warehouses and cloud platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks)

ETL tool experience (e.g., dbt, Matillion, Informatica, Fivetran)

Knowledge of AI/ML concepts and their role in data platforms

Degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field (preferred)

What We Offer:

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.

Do you want a job that looks different every day? Have you got an unquenchable passion for data and problem solving? Are you ready to jump into a work environment where you'll collaborate with some of the sharpest minds in the industry? We've got a career you'll enjoy.

At InterWorks, we like our work with a hefty side of play. Our team of high achievers is looking for an eager learner who doesn't shy away from hard work. We need someone whose overall data and programming savvy outweighs niche experience with a particular database platform. Whether you've worked primarily in SQL Server, Postgres, or a more modern cloud-based warehouse, all we really care about is that you have a strong understanding of building data pipelines and are ready to keep learning, every single day.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the InterWorks Sydney office in Sydney, NSW.  Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.  Must be a current resident of Australia to apply.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications:  $130,000-$150,000 AUD

What You’ll Do

Tackle diverse projects ranging in duration from a few days to a few months, for clients ranging from local Perth businesses to global enterprises and ASX-listed companies

Work with disparate data sources (relational databases, flat files, Excel, HDFS/Big Data systems, high-performance analytical databases, etc.) to unify client data

Collaborate closely with users to understand their unique needs and support them with the best solutions

Solve data-acquisition, integration, and management problems

Create ETL processes based on client needs while managing client expectations

What You’ll Need

Must-Haves

Excellent SQL fluency

Strong ETL proficiency using GUI-based tools or code-based patterns

Understanding of data-modelling principles

Passion for delivering compelling solutions that exceed client expectations

Excellent verbal and written communication skills

Strong problem-solving skills

Ability to translate business problems into practical data solutions and work effectively with non-technical stakeholders

A genuine thirst to learn

Adaptability and flexibility in changing situations

3+ years in a similar role

What We’d Like You to Have

Experience with modern data-engineering practices and frameworks

Experience with integration from semi-structured file and API sources

Familiarity with tools such as Informatica (IDMC), Matillion, Fivetran, or dbt

Experience with cloud platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery, or Azure Synapse

What We Offer:

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.

Ad Text:
Passionate about education?

Want to help improve outcomes for schools and students?

Reviews and School Improvement (RSI) provides system monitoring and assurance to support continuous improvement in Queensland state schools and education centres by providing leadership and oversight for targeted insights reviews. In addition, the RSI contributes significantly to distilling and sharing evidence-based insights and effective practices, to inform school improvement across the Queensland education system. Insights reviews (behaviour) will provide schools with practical guidance to strengthen their discipline systems and practices, enabling every school to deliver excellence through maintaining high expectations, a positive learning culture and quality teaching and learning.

The successful candidate will be employed at the classification listed on the attached role description under EOSD provisions.

About the Role

RSI are seeking Principals, Insights Reviews (behaviour) with particular knowledge and experience in leading effective and responsive school systems and practices which support student engagement for learning and providing effective support and interventions for student with diverse learning needs and complex behaviours.

As the Principal, Insights Reviews you will:

Lead insights reviews (behaviour) across Queensland State Schools to inform school and system improvement.
Play a key role in the delivery of the insights reviews as a team member.
Contribute to development of insights reviews methodology and processes.

Responsibilities include:

Deliver insights reviews, aligned to system priorities and initiatives, using evidence-based tools and methodologies.
Conduct insights reviews in line with agreed methodologies and processes.
As chair, lead teams, including the support and mentoring of review team members.
Capture high-quality evidence in reports that generate insights to inform school, region and system decision making.
Identify and share effective practice.
Collaboratively develop and implement approaches responsive to current and emerging system priorities.
Foster high quality collaboration, communication, cultural awareness and information sharing to enable an understanding of the complexities and challenges that exist in specific targeted areas.
Develop a rapport and engage with a range of external and internal stakeholders including Principals, school leaders, teachers, school staff, students, parents, regional staff and carers and community members to effectively and collaboratively enable an integrated approach to deliver the vision of the department.
Provide advice and expertise to improve school systems and processes that promote engagement and educational outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.
Understand and apply the legislation and policies that influence schooling especially in relation to students with diverse and/or complex learning needs and young people who are not actively engaged in a learning pathway.
Develop, articulate and promote a shared educational vision focused on providing quality learning outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.

The timing and location of school reviews can be a determining factor in when the employee is appointed.

Successful applicants will be expected to attend training prior to undertaking the role.

Applicants must be available to travel, including overnight absences.

Mandatory Requirements:

You must be a permanent employee of the department with a substantive teaching or classified officer role employed under Teaching in State Education Award - State 2016, have current full registration as a teacher and should be aware that temporary internal moves for the purposes of the department's payroll processes are referred to as internal secondments
You must be currently employed as a Principal or Acting Principal in a Queensland state school
Employees engaged temporarily as Education Officer - Special Duties have a requirement of 36¼ hours per week attendance and arrangements as outlined in clause 7.4 of the Department of Education State School Teachers Certified Agreement 2019

Interested? To Apply: 

Attach a brief resume (maximum 2 pages) including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history)
Attach a maximum two-page response demonstrating your experiences in leading a school improvement agenda, utilising strong interpersonal skills to be an effective leader, and your suitability for the role
Completion of the Principal, Insights Reviews questionnaire (Please note: once you have submitted your application documents and pressed next, you will be prompted to answer the required questionnaire)

Your new opportunity
Queensland Treasury plays a central role in supporting the Government's infrastructure and investment priorities. We are seeking a Principal Commercial Analyst to join our Commercial Project Advisory team within the Infrastructure Projects group.

In this role, you will work on complex and high-profile commercial matters, supporting government engagement with major infrastructure projects and private sector partners. You will contribute to practical, well-considered advice that informs investment decisions and delivery approaches across government, helping ensure projects are affordable, viable and deliver long-term value for Queenslanders.

This is an opportunity to apply your commercial expertise in a trusted central agency environment, where your work will directly influence outcomes of state-wide significance.

In this role, you will:
• Support the delivery of high-quality commercial, financial and transaction advice to government agencies and Treasury, particularly in relation to investment decisions, business cases, procurement and delivery models.
• Contribute to the development and assessment of delivery strategies, including Public Private Partnerships and other commercial models, by analysing risks, market capability and commercial structures.
• Analyse financial and commercial documentation such as feasibility assessments, financial models, cost estimates and demand forecasts to support robust, evidence-based recommendations.
• Support commercial assurance activities by constructively challenging assumptions, methodologies and commercial positions to strengthen project proposals.
• Build and manage effective working relationships with stakeholders across government and industry to support market engagement, negotiations and coordinated advice.
• Assist in resolving complex issues by contributing to timely, practical and outcome-focused solutions, including leading or supporting responses when issues arise.
• Build strong networks across government and industry to support successful project outcomes.

What experience you will bring:
• Demonstrated commercial experience in infrastructure, investment or major project environments, with a strong focus on value for money, affordability and appropriate risk allocation.
• Strong commercial acumen within a government, regulatory or policy context.
• Highly developed stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to work effectively across government, central agencies and industry to support coordinated, outcome-focused advice.
• The ability to apply commercial judgement in a government setting, synthesising financial, economic and strategic inputs to support sound investment decisions.

Whilst there are no mandatory qualifications needed for this role, relevant qualifications will be highly regarded.

How to apply
To submit your application, click on the Apply link to provide:
• Your resume detailing relevant experience
• A cover letter of no more than two pages outlining why you are interested in this position and how your knowledge, skills and experience makes you a strong candidate for the role.

Queensland Treasury is committed to building an inclusive and diverse workforce that reflects the community we serve and treating all applicants equitably. This starts with recruitment, including making reasonable adjustments to support applicants at all stages of the process.

Applications to remain current for 12 months
